title: Great Culture, Difficult to Grow
pros: Depending on your team, you can have a lot of fun. Some amazing people work at this company. The benefits on campus are also hard to beat, with free gyms, events, and visits from athletes. I can't speak for the whole company, but I had a good work-life balance in my team.
cons: It's quite difficult to get promoted within your team or organization, even if you're doing work above your band level. I've learned that it is also not as easy as it used to be to move around the company for a better position or more experience. Overall, it can become easy to feel stuck.

title: Greatness to Mediocrity
pros: Great benefits and PTO policy. Flexible work environment. Pay is generally good. Great peers and colleagues. Workout opportunities at campus. Buildings and facilities infrastructure at WHQ is beautiful.
cons: Until 2021, NIKE was a generally a great place to work. It has been going downhill since. Extremely political culture and lack of any good processes in the organization. Decisions by leadership are often based on favoritism, not merit. Company's direction keeps changing every quarter. Nike has experienced significant loss of talent and employee morale over the years. Besides select leaders, most of the leadership lacks GRIT to get through to the difficult times.

title: Work here for a good time, not a long time.
body: Work here for a good time, not a long time.
pros: Nike offers strong compensation, benefits, and brand visibility that can meaningfully accelerate your career. Youâll work alongside talented designers and cross-functional partners, and the work can be creatively fulfilling when priorities are clear. Flexibility exists in many teams, though itâs largely manager-dependent. The company name alone opens doors.
cons: Change is constantâoften without clear rationale, communication, or transition planning. Reorgs, shifting priorities, and leadership changes can make long-term growth paths feel uncertain. Decision-making can be slow, and strategy sometimes changes mid-stream, which impacts morale and momentum. Success here requires high adaptability and tolerance for ambiguity.

title: Great place to chill, not a good place if you care about growth/efficiency
body: Great place to chill, not a good place if you care about growth/efficiency
pros: - Fun product teams, cool events, good environment - Good benefits (competitive healthcare, time off, bonus structure, not amazing pay for an early career professional at a big company) - Networking opportunities - Work life balance STRONG, this is only in certain groups, other groups are extremely intense
cons: - Bad organization, no clear strategies - Hard to grow without leaving your team or moving to a new part of the business, very few natural opportunities for promotion - Pay is better than many companies considering the location but isn't great compared to larger companies in the US - Hard as a fresh out of college grad, not much guidance or mentoring, very much a sink or swim attitude

title: Itâs who you know, not what you know.
body: Itâs who you know, not what you know.
pros: Great benefits (health, dental, etc). Very chill work environment. Flexible schedule. Good time off policy. Sabbatical after 10 years (if you can last that long). Some EV charging. Great athletic facilities.
cons: Constant reorgs, with no security or stability. Not a meritocracy and hyper political. No consideration given to expertise, experience, or career goals during reorgs. Need to love the brand, not what you do, in order to be satisfied working at Nike. Inconsistent pay, tied to politics.

pros: Good pay, inflated title, everyone there is actually working at a level lower than their title. WHQ is an incredibly beautiful campus with super cool buildings. Lots of teambuilding/HH's, often on campus.
cons: SUPER political, completely vague decision making processes, all about the story being good/sexy, the financials are usually an after-thought or just made up and not challenged. No real accountability to deliver, ineffective people are often shuffled around instead of dealt with. Hell, sometimes their even promoted just to get them out of a key position. It's a marketing company with historically huge margins that has grown astronomically, you can kind of guess where that leads... It's mostly run by hired gun consultants even though Sr. Leadership is vastly overstaffed.

title: Behind the Swoosh: Innovation, Culture, and the Cost of the Cool
body: Behind the Swoosh: Innovation, Culture, and the Cost of the Cool
pros: Global Impact A brand that shapes sport, culture, and design on a global scale. Wellness-Driven Environment Nike promotes a strong wellness cultureâphysical, mental, and emotional. From wellness stipends to meditation apps and employee assistance programs, self-care is woven into the ethos. Onsite Amenities (HQ Perks) At Nike World Headquarters in Portland, you get access to gyms, running trails, wellness centers, cafes, and even childcareâall of which make it easier to integrate life and work. Supportive Time-Off Culture Generous PTO, paid holidays, sabbaticals, and mental health days are encouraged, not just offered. Leaders are usually supportive of taking time when you need it. Family-Friendly Policies Parental leave is robust (often exceeding U.S. standards), and thereâs strong support for caregivers, including resources for new parents and family support programs. Career Growth Opportunities With a big internal ecosystem, Nike supports lateral moves, promotions, mentorship, and global mobility.

title: They will work you to the bone and give nothing in return
body: They will work you to the bone and give nothing in return
pros: You get to work for the biggest shoe company in the world. Great People in the immediate teams. Great Benefits Great perks like company discounts, and facilities on campus
cons: Work-Life balance is nonexistent even though it is talked about daily. As with most large corporations you are not human to them, you are simply a number in their bottom line, and they will try whatever they can to get as much work out of you without compensating you for it. There is zero career growth despite how desperate this narrative is pushed. Market value salaries are low compared to other companies, because they know they can get away with it. It is not the same Nike it used to be.

title: Exciting Company to Work at however they have a bias against Women in my opinion
body: Exciting Company to Work at however they have a bias against Women in my opinion
pros: Good Training Fun Workplace environment (enjoyed most of my coworkerd had a positive attitude.) Understanding Management generally.
cons: Favourtism and extreme biases even in their policies against women. As a woman working at an entry level position I noticed there were no women in management except 1 where I worked. It was very male dominated. I worked there for years however my seniority was not valued and I was never offered opportunities to move up like my male counterparts. I was also forced to resign according to "Company Policy" because I dated one of my coworkers. I was often given the low level tasks the male employees did not want while they got the "fun" jobs.

title: Entitled Management

cons: Management will pressure you into getting memberships, have an obvious bias towards other employees, and be unprofessional/toxic overall. Some were okay, but others were just downright unbearable to be around and made the experience of working there more stressful than it needed to be. If you're used to working in retail, you'll be fine; otherwise, I'd recommend you stay away from working at this location.

title: Performative Values and Continued Discrimination
pros: Broad range of disciplines can offer exposure and career pivots for self-starters (as the company's HR function and most of its managers do not proactively nurture career growth)
cons: The company's strength is storytellingâthe problem is when it weaves a compelling story externally about its progressive values, only to be undermined by continued discriminatory behavior internally. The publicly-documented culture of gender discrimination, boys club, and toxic leadership behaviors is still alive and well. There is an ingrained culture of promoting charismatic career-climbers in leadership positions who do not prioritize nor understand true people management. Gratuitous self-promotion is necessary for advancement, not merit. The company cares about its bottom lineânot its people, not society, not the planet. Many employees at the company may care about sustainability, inclusivity, equity, and other areas of social impact, but the company's DNA and its executive leadership are grounded in achieving commercial results, even if at the expense of other stakeholders.

title: A great place to grow!
pros: Working at Nike has been an incredibly rewarding experience. The company fosters a collaborative, inclusive culture focused on innovation and growth. There are great opportunities for career development, with strong mentorship and a supportive leadership team. Nike values work-life balance, offering flexible schedules and wellness programs. The benefits package is excellent, with comprehensive health coverage and exclusive product perks. I appreciate the company's commitment to diversity, sustainability, and social responsibility. title: Chaotic, no FAANG money, but good work-life balance
body: Chaotic, no FAANG money, but good work-life balance
pros: Work-life balance is pretty great. Half-day Fridays in the summer and an additional (paid) week off each year for "wellness". Work can be exciting as an engineer, especially if you like working with cutting-edge cloud computing platform work (AWS).
cons: Pay is not greatânot even close to a big "tech company". However, what work-life balance would be like at those places is a gamble. Last few years have been very chaotic at an org-level. The company is struggling post-COVID and it remains to be seen how things shake out. Senior leadership is still a tangled mess, and they're getting their bearings.

title: Great Company Culture Going Through Tough Transition Period
body: Great Company Culture Going Through Tough Transition Period
pros: * Great corporate culture with amazing people who are nice and kind. * The employee benefits are amazing. * You can make a career out of working there.
cons: * The company is going through a significant transition period. Expect a lot of changes. * It takes a long time to implement any changes. * Some people are suffering from nostalgia syndrome and are not focusing on innovation; others stopped performing once they joined the company because they thought they "made it."
